summary: Polish pass cut 881 words, tightened duplicated scene reporting, and removed leftover abstract/documenty phrasing without changing beats.

wordcount: Opening ridge paragraph explained Millhaven's wrongness twice; trimmed the commander-pricing gloss and hinge detail.
Horse unease paragraph stacked three separate danger signals before Jaina even moved; reduced it to one horse beat plus the staff pulse.
Sweep setup over-described Farstrider lane coverage after the order already established the tactical pattern.
First-house inventory named abandonment timing twice through loaf detail and dust detail; compressed to one clean abandonment read.
Cyndia and Lyriel both delivered full village-empty reports; kept both voices but cut duplicate evidence.
Granary entry repeated stillness and market-day interruption across three consecutive sentences; tightened to one image cluster.
Spoor-reading section restated why a bad warning was dangerous after the point had already landed; cut the second explanation.
Final wagon departure and southbound reset repeated the same reduced-margin fact in multiple sentences; compressed the exit.
hitlist: Cut an eye/gaze cluster around Sylvanas, Jaina, Miri, and the trader; swapped several look beats for action or body-position beats.
Replaced 'Something brought it in' with 'Someone brought it in' so the sentence names agency instead of leaning on a vague placeholder.
Changed 'looked once' and 'looked up' beats to cleaner physical anchors to reduce repetitive camera language.
Changed 'committed' to 'gave the order' so Sylvanas's decision lands in character instead of abstract summary.
Changed 'quick field notation' to 'quick ranger marks' to pull the phrasing back inside the scene.
Trimmed document-adjacent phrasing in the dispatch setup while preserving the actual written report's formal function.
Reduced stacked descriptor load in house and wagon descriptions by cutting surplus setup detail.
Removed a redundant reaction sentence after Miri says the dead rose; the line itself already carries the beat.
Kept the three remaining 'like' uses because they stay under cap and each belongs to speaker/POV voice rather than filler comparison.
Left isolated 'pressed' uses alone because they are concrete physical contacts, not a chapter-wide contact-verb dominance problem.
dialogue: Sylvanas's spoken orders stayed clipped and operational; no bureaucratic or memo-register vocabulary remained in her dialogue.
Jaina's plague explanation stayed plain enough for field speech; no vector/diagnostic/clinical jargon survived the polish pass.
Neral's cellar talk to Miri remained grounded and humane, not therapeutic or explanatory-overwritten.
Miri's witness line keeps child-voice simplification; the comparison about forgotten legs reads as frightened observation, not AI ornament.
Trader dialogue stayed mercantile and local, with road knowledge instead of exposition-dump formality.
The dispatch paragraph was left formal on purpose because it is an in-scene written message, not spoken dialogue or narrator seep.
No fake-simplification exchange triggered in quoted speech; all explanatory lines cash out into route, source, risk, or action.

changes.before: From the ridge Sylvanas had read Millhaven as cold chimneys and silence, the wrongness a field commander could price from half a mile off.
changes.after: From the ridge Sylvanas had read Millhaven as cold chimneys and silence.

changes.before: Behind her the Thalassian horses pinned their ears flat... Even the elven mounts... wanted nothing from this ground.
changes.after: Even the Thalassian horses wanted nothing from this ground.

changes.before: They moved through Millhaven's muddy lanes in Farstrider rhythm, one forward and one covering, angles crossing...
changes.after: They moved in Farstrider rhythm, one forward and one covering.

changes.before: Rough Lordaeron boards under her boots... The loaf sat on a bread board near the stove...
changes.after: River-stone hearth, smoke-black rafters... Dust lay on the bucket bail by the hearth...

changes.before: The blacksmith's tongs are still clamped... The tanner's yard has hides pegged...
changes.after: Meals left out. Beds slept in. Work abandoned in the middle of it. Fourteen doors open and not one body.

changes.before: Sylvanas looked once at the mill...
A warning sent on bad direction was worse than none. It pulled riders to the wrong road and left the right one open.
changes.after: A warning sent on bad direction was worse than none.

changes.before: No one in the room moved when she said it.
changes.after: Removed so Miri's line lands without a narrator footnote.

changes.before: quick field notation from a ranger...
changes.after: quick ranger marks from a woman...

changes.before: Sylvanas looked once more at the broken crust, then committed.
changes.after: Sylvanas took one last measure of the broken crust, then gave the order.

changes.before: If you do not meet a patrol, ride until you do.
changes.after: If you do not, ride until you do.

changes.before: Neral kept one hand on the mare's girth while she spoke, letting the horse feel her there.
changes.after: Neral kept one hand on the mare's girth while she spoke.

changes.before: Jaina mounted without asking where they were going, settling the staff across her thighs with mud still drying dark on the hem of her robes and grain dust caught at one sleeve.
changes.after: Jaina mounted without asking where they were going, the staff across her thighs.

flags: Dispatch paragraph kept formal because it is a written field report inside the scene, not narrator document voice.
Three remaining 'like' uses were kept: Sylvanas's judgment at line 59, Miri's child-voice comparison at line 139, and the trader's ordinary spoken comparison at line 265.
